scenario: An Indian exporter, M/s ABC Exports, has submitted a bill for USD 100,000 to their bank for realization on 10th May 2026. The inter-bank USD/INR spot rate is 83.20/25. The 1-month forward premium is 10/12 points. The bank requires an exchange margin of 0.15% on the buying rate. The transit period for the bill is 25 days (round up to 1 month for forward premium application). The customer wants the funds credited to their CC account today.

questions.question: What is the base Spot Buying rate the bank will use?
questions.explanation: In USD/INR 83.20/25, the lower rate (83.20) is the bid/buying rate and the higher rate (83.25) is the ask/selling rate.

questions.question: Calculate the applicable Forward Premium (1-month).
questions.explanation: For buying rates, the lower premium (10 points) is used. 10 points = 0.0010.

questions.question: What is the rate after adding the forward premium but BEFORE the exchange margin?
questions.explanation: Spot Buying (83.20) + Forward Premium (0.01) = 83.21. Wait, 10 points is 0.0010, so 83.20 + 0.0010 = 83.2010. Let me check the math. Yes, 83.2010.

questions.question: Calculate the Exchange Margin amount (0.15% of the rate).
What is the final TT Buying Rate (rounded to 4 decimals)?
